prompt:
Read AGENTS.md first — it has mandatory workflow constraints and PR requirements for this repo.

Task: add declarative bare-git hosting to the classic-laddie host (hosts/classic-laddie) so it can become the canonical origin for the owner's repos. This is Phase 0 of a source-hosting migration off GitHub; the pilot repo is called "the-valley". Hosting only — no web UI, no CI, no event bus.

What to build:
1. A reusable NixOS module (under modules/, following this repo's module conventions — look at modules/klaus-worker and modules/media-server for the house style) providing:
   - A dedicated git user with git-shell (no interactive login), SSH-key auth only. Identity stays deliberately thin: the box is on the tailnet, so Tailscale ACLs plus SSH keys are the whole identity story for now.
   - Bare repositories under a dedicated directory on the host's ZFS pool (check hosts/classic-laddie for existing dataset/mount conventions and follow them).
   - A declarative list of repos (e.g. services.git-hosting.repos = [ "the-valley" ]) — the module ensures <name>.git exists as a bare repo with correct ownership on activation. It must never delete or overwrite an existing repo; removing a name from the list leaves the data in place.
   - A per-repo hook mechanism (a hooks directory or templated post-receive) so a replication hook can be dropped in later. The actual offsite mirror (a Hetzner VPS that does not exist yet) is a follow-up — scaffold the hook wiring, do not invent remote endpoints.
2. Enable the module on classic-laddie with the-valley as the first repo.
3. Docs: a short docs/git-hosting.md covering how to add a repo, how to clone/push (ssh to the git user on classic-laddie over the tailnet), and where the hooks live.

Acceptance:
- nix flake check passes and the classic-laddie system config builds (match however AGENTS.md / this repo's CI builds host configs).
- The module is enabled in hosts/classic-laddie with repos = [ "the-valley" ].
- No secrets committed. If something genuinely needs a secret, note it in the PR description instead of inventing agenix plumbing.

Do NOT deploy anything — this is a config PR only; the owner deploys. Open a PR; do not merge. Lead the PR description with the module's option surface (the options and their defaults) so the owner can review the shape quickly.
